file_insights.changes_summary: Added two major model classes (Subscription and Invoice) with comprehensive billing lifecycle management
key_additions: Subscription model with 18+ fields covering billing states, trial management, and payment provider integration
Invoice model with detailed billing record tracking and payment status management
Multiple properties for subscription state checking (is_active, is_valid, is_trialing, etc.)
Methods for subscription lifecycle management (activate, cancel, expire, mark_past_due)
Invoice-related methods for payment processing and status transitions
Database indexes optimized for common queries (org_status, trial_ends, billing dates)
Comprehensive docstrings with lifecycle diagrams and usage examples

patterns_discovered.description: Both Subscription and Invoice models include detailed docstrings with lifecycle diagrams, attribute descriptions, usage examples, and critical rules
patterns_discovered.evidence: Full lifecycle explanation in docstrings with state transitions (TRIALING → ACTIVE → PAST_DUE → CANCELLED/EXPIRED/SUSPENDED)

patterns_discovered.description: Consistent use of SoftDeleteMixin and SoftDeleteManager throughout models, with explicit guidance against direct delete() calls
patterns_discovered.evidence: Both models inherit TimeStampedMixin and SoftDeleteMixin; uses objects and all_objects managers

patterns_discovered.description: Fields designed for third-party payment provider integration (Iyzico) with external_subscription_id and external_customer_id
patterns_discovered.evidence: Multiple fields for payment provider IDs with db_index for performance; payment_details JSON field for masked card info

patterns_discovered.description: Multiple boolean properties and status checks to determine subscription validity and access permissions
patterns_discovered.evidence: Properties: is_active, is_valid, is_trialing, is_cancelled, is_expired, is_past_due, is_suspended

patterns_discovered.description: Strategic multi-field and single-field indexes on frequently queried combinations
patterns_discovered.evidence: 7 indexes on Subscription covering org_status, status_period, status_billing, trial_ends, external IDs, deleted_at

patterns_discovered.description: All verbose_name, help_text, and string outputs use gettext_lazy (_) for translation support
patterns_discovered.evidence: Consistent use of _() for all user-facing strings throughout both models

patterns_discovered.description: Distinct fields for trial_ends_at, current_period_start/end, and next_billing_date enable flexible billing scenarios
patterns_discovered.evidence: Trial management separate from billing period tracking; next_billing_date calculated for renewal

patterns_discovered.description: Two cancellation modes: cancel_at_period_end (deferred) vs immediate cancellation with optional reason capture
patterns_discovered.evidence: cancel_at_period_end boolean field; cancel_reason text field; cancel() method with both options

patterns_discovered.description: Use of @property decorators for derived values avoiding database hits
patterns_discovered.evidence: trial_remaining_days, days_until_renewal, formatted_price, and status check properties

patterns_discovered.description: Invoice model tracks payment attempts with multiple status transitions and retry logic
patterns_discovered.evidence: Invoice fields: status, attempt_count, last_attempt_at, next_retry_at, payment_error_message

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Subscription Status vs Soft Delete Distinction
gotchas_discovered.description: Model has both status field (ACTIVE, EXPIRED, etc.) and deleted_at field from SoftDeleteMixin. Deleted subscriptions are completely hidden by default.
gotchas_discovered.implication: Queries must account for both - status queries with active records, vs soft-deleted recovery using all_objects manager

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Trial Period Overlap with Billing Period
gotchas_discovered.description: Both trial_ends_at and current_period_end can exist simultaneously; they serve different purposes but must be carefully synchronized
gotchas_discovered.implication: After trial expiration, system must transition to ACTIVE with new current_period_end; validation logic needed to prevent desync

gotchas_discovered.description: Subscription has on_delete=models.PROTECT for Plan FK, preventing plan deletion if subscriptions exist
gotchas_discovered.implication: Must soft-delete plans or manage subscription migration before hard deleting plans

gotchas_discovered.description: Subscription uses on_delete=models.CASCADE for Organization, automatically deleting all subscriptions when org is deleted
gotchas_discovered.implication: Hard deletion of organizations will lose billing history; soft-delete pattern should extend to organizations

gotchas_discovered.description: Invoice model has strict status progression (DRAFT → PENDING → PAID/FAILED/CANCELLED) with specific field requirements per status
gotchas_discovered.implication: Status transition validation must prevent invalid transitions; some transitions may only be allowed from specific states

gotchas_discovered.description: cancel_at_period_end determines whether status becomes CANCELLED (deferred) or EXPIRED (immediate), but both set cancelled_at
gotchas_discovered.implication: is_cancelled property checks both status and cancelled_at, which may be inconsistent if cancel() is bypassed

gotchas_discovered.description: trial_remaining_days property returns 0 for non-trialing subscriptions, making it unsuitable for trial-specific logic without is_trialing check
gotchas_discovered.implication: Callers must use is_trialing + trial_remaining_days pattern; single property alone is insufficient

gotchas_discovered.description: external_subscription_id and external_customer_id lack unique_together constraint; duplicate external IDs could cause payment processing confusion
gotchas_discovered.implication: No database-level enforcement; application must prevent duplicate external IDs or add unique constraints

gotchas_discovered.description: next_billing_date is independent field not auto-calculated from current_period_end; must be manually set and kept in sync
gotchas_discovered.implication: Manual synchronization logic required; out-of-sync dates could trigger premature/delayed billing

approach_outcome.completion_details: Successfully implemented two interconnected billing models with complete lifecycle management, state tracking, and payment provider integration
key_achievements: Subscription model with all necessary billing state fields and properties
Invoice model with payment tracking and retry logic
Comprehensive property-based API for status checking and derived values
Database schema optimized with strategic indexes
Clear documentation of billing lifecycle and usage patterns
approach_outcome.code_quality: High - extensive docstrings, consistent patterns, proper use of Django best practices
approach_outcome.testing_readiness: Model structure ready; requires unit tests for state transitions, property calculations, and edge cases
approach_outcome.integration_ready: Models designed for Iyzico payment provider integration with external ID fields and payment details JSON storage

recommendations.recommendation: Add unique_together constraint for (organization, status, deleted_at) to enforce one active subscription per organization
recommendations.rationale: Prevents multiple active subscriptions for single organization, which would create billing conflicts

recommendations.recommendation: Implement clean() method on Subscription to validate state transitions (e.g., only ACTIVE/TRIALING can transition to CANCELLED)
recommendations.rationale: Prevents invalid status transitions at application level

recommendations.recommendation: Add automatic sync logic between trial_ends_at and current_period_end during activate()
recommendations.rationale: Ensures billing period starts correctly after trial expiration

recommendations.recommendation: Add select_related/prefetch_related hints in Meta.managers for organization and plan queries
recommendations.rationale: Reduces N+1 queries when fetching subscriptions with related data

recommendations.recommendation: Add status change logging to AuditLog model in all state transition methods (activate, cancel, expire, mark_past_due)
recommendations.rationale: Creates complete audit trail for billing changes; critical for compliance and debugging

recommendations.recommendation: Create test cases for: trial expiration, period renewal, cancellation modes, grace period past_due flow, invoice payment retries
recommendations.rationale: Billing logic is critical; comprehensive tests prevent revenue-impacting bugs

recommendations.recommendation: Create migration guide documenting how to handle upgrading/downgrading subscriptions and prorating changes
recommendations.rationale: Current model handles basic lifecycle but lacks guidance for common billing operations

recommendations.recommendation: Add webhook handlers for Iyzico payment events (succeeded, failed, refunded) that automatically update Invoice status
recommendations.rationale: External payment events must drive Invoice state; ensures system stays in sync with payment provider

recommendations.recommendation: Add error_message field to Invoice for payment failures; implement exponential backoff in retry_payment logic
recommendations.rationale: Provides visibility into payment failures; prevents excessive retry attempts overwhelming payment provider

recommendations.recommendation: Verify SoftDeleteMixin implementation includes deleted_at field and verify all soft-delete queries use .objects (not all_objects)
recommendations.rationale: Ensures soft-delete pattern is consistently applied; prevents accidental exposure of deleted billing data
